title: Poa fenas
intro: Poa fenas, commonly known as Meadow Grass or Meadow Foxtail, is a perennial grass species found in temperate regions worldwide. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is used to treat various health conditions such as digestive issues and skin problems. The bioactive constituents of Poa fenas include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Poa fenas include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Poa fenas is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Pooideae and tribe Poeae. The genus Poa is a large and widespread group of grasses, often referred to as meadow grasses or meadow foxtails. Poa fenas is native to the northern hemisphere, particularly in Europe, Asia, and North America. This species is commonly found in moist, grassy areas and is often associated with woodland edges and hedgerows.

parts: Poa fenas, also known as Poa pratensis, is a type of grass that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries.
